mappings.rationale: Directly covers Judaism including its history, beliefs, practices, institutions, and present-day forms — the primary subject of section 826.
Covers the Bible including the Hebrew Bible/Old Testament, directly relevant to 826.B.1 on biblical literature.
Directly covers the Old Testament/TaNaKh, the foundational scripture of Judaism, relevant to 826.B.1.b on Torah, Nevi'im, Ketuvim.
Examines the Hebrew Bible as literature, directly relevant to 826.B.1 on biblical literature of Judaism.
Directly covers the Qumran literature (Dead Sea Scrolls), the specific subject of 826.B.2.
Covers early Jewish-Christian texts relevant to understanding the relationship between Judaism and early Christianity, relevant to 826.B.1.c on noncanonical literature.
Directly covers the history of Judaism across all eras, relevant to 826.A on the history of Judaism.
Covers Jewish literary traditions, relevant to 826.B on intellectual, spiritual, and imaginative expressions of Judaism.
Directly covers Jewish mysticism (Kabbalah), relevant to 826.B.5 on mystical and devotional writings.
Covers archaeological evidence related to biblical history and the land of Israel, relevant to 826.A.1 on the biblical era and 826.C.2.d on the land of Israel.
Covers the history of antisemitism and its impact on Jewish communities, relevant to 826.A.4-5 on the medieval and modern eras of Judaism.
Covers Zionism as a movement deeply connected to Jewish identity and the concept of the land of Israel, relevant to 826.A.5 on developments in modern Judaism and 826.C.2.d on sacred places.
Covers the Israeli-Palestinian conflict which is deeply connected to Jewish religious claims to the land of Israel, relevant to 826.C.2.d on the land of Israel and Jerusalem.
Covers Judaism in comparative perspective with Christianity and Islam, relevant to understanding Judaism's place among the Abrahamic traditions.
Covers theological approaches relevant to Jewish theology and doctrines concerning God described in 826.C.1.a.
Covers concepts of God including the Jewish understanding of monotheism, relevant to 826.C.1.a on doctrines concerning God.
Covers interpretive theory relevant to 826.B.4 on Judaic exegesis and hermeneutics.
Covers Judaism in the American context, relevant to understanding modern forms of Judaism (Reform, Conservative, Reconstructionism) in 826.C.3.
The Crusades significantly affected Jewish communities in Europe, relevant to 826.A.4 on the medieval era of Judaism.
Covers the history of the Jewish ghetto as an institution that shaped Jewish communal life and religious practice, relevant to 826.A.4 on the medieval era.
Paul's theology emerged from and engaged with Judaism, relevant to understanding the Hellenistic era of Judaism in 826.A.2.
Covers diaspora communities with significant attention to the Jewish diaspora, relevant to 826.A and the demographic aspects of Judaism in 826.A.6.
The Holocaust profoundly impacted Judaism and Jewish communities, relevant to 826.A.5 on developments in modern Judaism.
The Enlightenment shaped the emergence of Reform Judaism and Jewish emancipation, relevant to 826.A.5 on developments in modern Judaism.
Maimonides is one of the most important figures in Jewish thought, author of the Mishneh Torah, directly relevant to this section on Judaism.
